zh.Of, Willesborough, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特維也斯波洛
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Clifton於大約1450年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾時，她的父親Sir Gervase Clifton39歲，母親Isabel Herbert Finch46歲。她於大約1470年與John Digges在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Of, Willesborough, Kent, England: 英国英格兰肯特維也斯波洛
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Clifton于大约1450年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾时，她的父亲Sir Gervase Clifton39岁，母亲Isabel Herbert Finch46岁。她于大约1470年与John Digges在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Clifton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the very numerous places called Clifton, from Old English clif ‘slope’ (see Cliff ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Cliff
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Cliff(e), Cle(e)ve, or Clive, from Old English clif ‘slope, bank, cliff’, or a topographic name from the same word. The Old English word was used not only in the sense of modern English cliff but also of much gentler slopes and frequently also of a riverbank or seashore.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Clifton was born about 1450, in Nottinghamshire, England, her father, Sir Gervase Clifton, was 39 and her mother, Isabel Herbert Finch, was 46. She married John Digges about 1470, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Clifton est née environ 1450, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Gervase Clifton, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Isabel Herbert Finch, avait 46 ans. Elle a épousé John Digges environ 1470, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Clifton um 1450 in Nottinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Gervase Clifton 39 und ihre Mutter Isabel Herbert Finch 46. Sie hat um 1470 in England John Digges geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Clifton è nata il circa 1450 nel Nottinghamshire, England, sua padre, Sir Gervase Clifton, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Isabel Herbert Finch, aveva 46 anni. Lei sposato John Digges circa 1470, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Cliftonが約1450年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Gervase Cliftonは39歳，母Isabel Herbert Finchは46歳であった。約1470年にイギリスでJohn Diggesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Clifton(이)가 대략 1450년 영국 노틴그함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Gervase Clifton(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Herbert Finch(은)는 46세였다. 그녀는 대략 1470년 영국에서 John Digges(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다.

mn.Of, Willesborough, Kent, England: Willesborough, Centlond, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Clifton nasceu aproximadamente 1450, em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Gervase Clifton, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Herbert Finch, tinha 46 anos. Ela casou-se com John Digges aproximadamente 1470, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Clifton родилась около 1450 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Gervase Clifton было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Herbert Finch, 46. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Digges около 1470 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Clifton nació aproximadamente 1450, en Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Gervase Clifton, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Isabel Herbert Finch, tenía 46 años. Se casó con John Digges aproximadamente 1470, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas.
